hero.title: 6 Days, the Sacred Triangle.
hero.intro: The spiritual triangle of the north. Varanasi's eternal ghats, Prayagraj's Triveni Sangam where three sacred rivers meet, and Ayodhya's Ram Mandir. Six days through the holiest geography in the Ganges plains.
hero.heroImageAlt: Varanasi Prayagraj Ayodhya sacred triangle pilgrimage

overview.heading: 6 Days India's Sacred Triangle: The Full Picture
body: India's Sacred Triangle links three of the holiest cities in Hinduism. Varanasi, the oldest living city on the Ganges; Prayagraj (Allahabad), where the Ganges, Yamuna and the mythical Saraswati meet at the Triveni Sangam; and Ayodhya, the birthplace of Lord Rama. Together they form a profound spiritual circuit.

This 6 day Sacred Triangle package from Malaysia is arranged privately with a professional guide. You experience Varanasi's sunrise boats and Ganga Aarti, the sacred confluence at Prayagraj, and Ayodhya's temples and Sarayu river, all linked by your own vehicle. It is the most complete spiritual journey through the Ganges heartland.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Varanasi's Sacred Ghats
items.body: The sunrise boat ride and the evening Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat, the essential experiences of the spiritual capital.

items.title: Triveni Sangam at Prayagraj
items.body: The sacred confluence of the Ganges, Yamuna and the mythical Saraswati rivers, site of the Kumbh Mela and one of the holiest places in India.

items.title: Ayodhya's Ram Mandir
items.body: The grand new Ram Janmabhoomi temple and the sacred sites of Lord Rama's birthplace complete the spiritual triangle.

whyBook.heading: The Sacred Triangle, Guided With Care
whyBook.body: Three holy cities, each with its own customs, linked by considerable driving. We route the journey and pair you with a guide who understands all three traditions. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India journeys since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: India's Sacred Triangle Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 6 day package is the most complete spiritual circuit of the Ganges plains, linking Varanasi, Prayagraj and Ayodhya, three of the holiest cities in Hinduism.

With a professional guide and a private vehicle, you experience Varanasi's ghats and aarti, the sacred confluence at Prayagraj, and Ayodhya's Ram Mandir, a profound and well-paced journey.

It is ideal for devotees and culture travellers who want the full sacred triangle in one trip.

sections.heading: The Triveni Sangam at Prayagraj
sections.body:
Prayagraj, formerly Allahabad, is home to the Triveni Sangam, the sacred meeting point of the Ganges, the Yamuna and the mythical Saraswati rivers. It is one of the holiest sites in India and the location of the Kumbh Mela, the largest religious gathering on earth.

A boat ride to the confluence, where the brown Ganges and green Yamuna visibly meet, is a moving experience and a highlight of the sacred triangle.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Varanasi or Ayodhya Airport (VNS / AYJ). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the Sacred Triangle?
items.answer: The Sacred Triangle links three of the holiest cities in Hinduism: Varanasi on the Ganges, Prayagraj at the Triveni Sangam confluence, and Ayodhya, the birthplace of Lord Rama. Together they form a deeply meaningful pilgrimage circuit.

items.question: What is the best time for this pilgrimage?
items.answer: October to March offers the most comfortable weather. The Kumbh and Magh Mela at Prayagraj draw immense crowds in winter. Festivals at Varanasi and Ayodhya are spectacular but busy. Winter is ideal for the boats and temples.

items.question: How much driving does this involve?
items.answer: The three cities are spread across the Ganges plains, so there is significant driving between them. The six-day pace and private vehicle keep this comfortable, with the route planned to be as efficient as possible.
